title: South Korean regulator begins sanctions process against Dunamu: Report
summary: The proceedings come as South Korea’s Virtual Asset User Protection Act lacks explicit sanctions provisions for hacking and computer system incidents, leaving the scope of penalties uncertain.

title: Electronic Transactions Association CEO Expecting More Partnerships with Bitcoin Startups
summary: Electronic Transactions Association (ETA) CEO, Jason Oxman, indicated that members of his organization might start recognizing Bitcoin’s disruptive potential, suggesting that this might lead to more partnerships between traditional electronic payment providers and Bitcoin startups.

title: US, Iran Launch Fresh Strikes After Two Troops Die in Jordan
summary: The US and Iran engaged in another wave of tit-for-tat attacks, signaling no let-up after strikes by the Islamic Republic killed two American service members in Jordan on Friday.

title: Caspian Pipeline Consortium Halts Loadings After Drone Strike
summary: Oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium’s terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast was halted after a drone strike.

title: ECB Warns Stablecoins May Drain Bank Deposits—Here's What That Means
summary: ECB board member Piero Cipollone laid out the three-layer threat banks face from digital payments, and pitched the digital euro as the only structural answer.

title: US regulators miss GENIUS Act’s one-year deadline for final stablecoin rules
summary: The delay does not postpone the law’s Jan. 18, 2027 effective date, leaving regulators and stablecoin issuers with a compressed implementation window.

title: Bitcoin Japan, which holds no bitcoin, taps EVO Fund in planned $60 million raise to finally buy some
summary: A prior December raise instead funded stakes tied to SpaceX and Figure AI, which the company says are its two AI infrastructure investments to date.
